title: Hakea geraldtonica
taxonomy: Hakea geraldtonica is a species of flowering plant in the family Proteaceae. It is native to Western Australia, specifically in the Geraldton Sandplains region. The plant is commonly known as the Geraldton yellow bells due to its bright yellow flowers. Hakea geraldtonica belongs to the genus Hakea, which consists of over 150 species of shrubs and trees found in Australia. The classification of Hakea geraldtonica is as follows: Kingdom: Plantae, Phylum: Magnoliophyta, Class: Magnoliopsida, Order: Proteales, Family: Proteaceae, Genus: Hakea, Species: H. geraldtonica.

intro: Hakea geraldtonica, commonly known as the Geraldton hakea, is a flowering plant species native to Western Australia, characterized by its showy white flowers and distinctive foliage. The plant has been traditionally used in Australian Indigenous medicine to treat a range of health issues, including respiratory problems and skin conditions, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. Bioactive constituents of Hakea geraldtonica include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which have been shown to possess antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities. Hakea geraldtonica is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and extracts, which are valued for their potential to support respiratory health and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu.
